body:
## Rationale
Metallica coverage on MetalForge:
- master-of-puppets-drum-setup ✅
- and-justice-for-all-drum-setup ✅
- black-album-drum-setup ✅
- aenima-drum-setup ✅ (wrong band — Tool)
- death-magnetic and later albums: likely in ai-fix
- **Kill 'Em All (1983) — NOT in DB, NOT in proposals**

Kill 'Em All is the debut album that launched thrash metal. Lars recorded it with a **basic Tama Imperial Star** — a budget 5-piece touring kit, no endorsements, no triggers. This is a completely distinct gear story from any other Metallica article: stock hardware, mixed-brand cymbals, zero custom elements.

The "before and after" narrative (1983 Tama Imperial Star → later DW/custom rigs) is unique and search-worthy. Captures "lars ulrich kill em all drum setup", "lars ulrich 1983 kit", "metallica debut album drums".

NOT in albumArticles.js. NOT in open proposals (mentioned as quality-rated in decisions log during a cap-hold, but never filed as a GitHub issue).

## Slug
`kill-em-all-drum-setup`

## Files to touch
- `packages/frontend/data/albumArticles.js` — add new entry
- `public/llms/articles/kill-em-all-drum-setup.md` — LLM companion

## Article data shape
```
slug: kill-em-all-drum-setup
drummer: Lars Ulrich
band: Metallica
album: Kill Em All
year: 1983
label: Megaforce Records
kit: Tama Imperial Star 5-piece (budget touring kit, natural finish)
snare: Tama wood snare 14x5.5 inch
cymbals: Mixed Paiste 2002 and Zildjian A (no formal endorsement, mix of available cymbals)
hardware: Tama standard hardware with Ludwig bass drum pedal
gear_narrative: Recorded in April 1983 at Rochester NY with essentially zero budget. Lars showed up with a bare-bones touring kit — no endorsement deals, no triggers, no custom hardware. The album raw sound is inseparable from this minimal setup. Stark contrast to Lars later Tama/Sonor signature rigs.
faq:
  - Q: What drum kit did Lars Ulrich use on Kill Em All?
    A: A basic Tama Imperial Star 5-piece touring kit, no endorsements...
  - Q: What cymbals did Lars Ulrich use on Kill Em All?
    A: A mixed set of Paiste 2002 and Zildjian A before any formal endorsement deals...
  - Q: How was Lars Ulrichs kit on Kill Em All different from later Metallica albums?
    A: Kill Em All used a budget Tama Imperial Star vs. later Tama custom and Sonor Signature setups...
relatedAlbums: [master-of-puppets-drum-setup, and-justice-for-all-drum-setup]
```

## Quality gate
- >= 300 words
- FAQPage JSON-LD >= 3 Q&As
- Article JSON-LD
- >= 3 internal links: Lars Ulrich profile, MoP article, AJFA article
- LLM file >= 150 words

## Estimated organic uplift
Targets: "kill em all drum setup", "lars ulrich 1983", "metallica debut drums", "lars ulrich first drum kit". Opens Metallica chronological arc at its origin (1983 → 1988 AJFA ✅ → 1991 MoP ✅). High name recognition + debut-album curiosity = strong TAM.

## Verify
1. `kill-em-all-drum-setup` routes at `/articles/kill-em-all-drum-setup`
2. FAQPage JSON-LD present in source
3. `/llms/articles/kill-em-all-drum-setup.md` returns 200
4. Internal links to Lars profile, MoP, AJFA work
5. Sitemap updated

## Done
When kill-em-all-drum-setup live with schema and LLM companion deployed.
title: SEO: Metallica 'Kill 'Em All' drum setup article (Lars Ulrich, 1983 — thrash metal birth article, opens Metallica arc at its origin)
